briefs.title: Burnaby to Appoint Chief Election Officer for 2026 Election
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby will appoint a Chief Election Officer to manage the 2026 general local election. This officer will be in charge of running the election according to local election laws.
briefs.source_body:
7.1
APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER
[image]
1.CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To recommend the appointment of the Chief Election
Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA Renewal Approved for 5 Years
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has approved the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for another five years. A BIA helps local businesses work together to improve their area. The city received enough support to move forward with Bylaw #14797.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Professional Services
briefs.source_body:
7.2
CERTIFICATE OF SUFFICIENCY: 2026 BURNABY
NORTH ROAD BIA RENEWAL
[image]
1.Certificate of Sufficiency North Road BIA Renewal
2026-04-14 FINAL (sufficient)_.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Certificate of Sufficiency (North Road BIA)
2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To provide results for the petitions submitted against
the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA)
renewal bylaw for a 5-year period.

see Bylaw #14797

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority: Shareholder Approval Needed for 2026 Corporate Maintenance
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) needs Council approval for annual shareholder corporate maintenance matters for 2026. This is a standard yearly process to keep the corporation in good standing.
briefs.action_required: No action required. This is for informational purposes only.
briefs.source_body:
7.3
BHA - SHAREHOLDER'S CORPORATE
MAINTENANCE MATTERS FOR 2026
[image]
1.BHA - Shareholder's Corporate Maintenance Matters for 2026
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval with respect to certain
annual shareholder’s corporate maintenance matters for 2026
on behalf of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation
(BHA).

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority Presents 2025 Financials, 2026 Budget
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) presented its annual financial statements for 2025 and its operating budget for 2026. These documents outline the BHA's financial performance and planned spending.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
BHA - 2025 FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND 2026
OPERATING BUDGET
[image]
1.BHA - 2026 Financial Statements 2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - BHA - 2025 Audited Financial Statements
2026-04-14 .pdf
3.Attachment 2 - BHA - 2026 Operating Budget 2026-04-14
.pdf
Purpose: To present the Burnaby Housing Authority’s (BHA)
2025 Annual Financial Statements and 2026 Annual Operating
Budget.

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers E-Bike, Scooter Bylaw Changes
briefs.summary: Burnaby is thinking about changing its rules for electric bike share programs and electric kick scooters. The city wants to update the Street and Traffic Bylaw and the Parks Regulation Bylaw to include these new transportation options. These changes are being proposed to manage how these services operate within Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed bylaw amendments to understand the new rules.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS TO ADDRESS E-
BIKE SHARE PILOT PROGRAM AND PROVINCIAL
ELECTRIC KICK SCOOTER PILOT PROGRAM
[image]
1.Prop. Bylaw Amendments for E-Bike Share and Prov. Electric

Kick Scooter Pilot Programs - 2026-04-14 .pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Proposed Bylaw Amendments (E-Bike Share
and Electric Kick Scooter) 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To propose amendments to the Burnaby Street and
Traffic Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to
implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the
provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the City.

briefs.title: Burnaby to Report Housing Target Progress to BC Ministry
briefs.summary: Burnaby must send a report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. The report details the city's progress toward meeting housing targets set by the province. This is required by the Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation.
briefs.action_required: No action is required from business owners.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body:
7.6
PROVINCIAL HOUSING TARGET ORDER 6-MONTH
INTERIM PROGRESS REPORT
[image]
1.Provincial Housing Target Order 6-Month Interim Progress
Report - 2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Provincial Housing Target Order
2026-04-14.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Housing Target Progress Report Form
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To present the Housing Target Order and obtain
Council authorization to forward Burnaby’s Housing Target
Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing and
Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act and
Housing Supply Regulation.

briefs.title: Building Benchmark BC Asks for Year 7 Participation Approval
briefs.summary: Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) is seeking approval to participate in Year 7 of the program. BBBC helps building owners compare their building's energy and water use to similar buildings. This helps owners find ways to save money and reduce their environmental impact.
briefs.action_required: Review the Building Benchmark BC program details.
briefs.source_body:
7.7
BUILDING BENCHMARK BC
[image]
1.Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) 2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Building Benchmark BC Annual Report 2024
(Year 5) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To update Council on Building Benchmark BC and
seek authorization to participate in Year 7.

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves FortisBC Gas Line Right-of-Way on Pandora and Gaglardi
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a statutory right-of-way (legal permission) for FortisBC to install and maintain natural gas lines on parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. This allows FortisBC to build and service gas infrastructure on those properties.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
7950 PANDORA ST AND 1200 GAGLARDI WAY -
BURNABY MOUNTAIN - STATUTORY RIGHT-OF-
WAY FOR FORTIS BC
[image]
1.7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way - Burnaby
Mountain Fortis SRW 2026-04-14 .pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 to 4 (7950 Pandora St. and 1200
Gaglardi Way) 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to grant Statutory Right-of-
Way in favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. (FortisBC) over a portion
of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way to permit the
installation, operations and maintenance of natural gas works.

briefs.title: Modular Housing Project Approved in BC
briefs.summary: A contract was approved for a pilot project to build a modular four-plex housing unit. This project aims to create more housing options.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - DEVELOPMENT OF MODULAR HOUSING
FOUR-PLEX AT 8315 14TH AVENUE
[image]
1.CA - Development Modular Housing Four-Plex Unit Final
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the development of a modular four-plex housing unit as a
pilot project.

briefs.title: Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Contract Approved
briefs.summary: The local council approved a contract for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. This means the project can move forward with the chosen contractor.
briefs.source_body:
7.10
CA - (CB-8039) JERRY ROGERS CREEK CULVERT
REPLACEMENT
[image]
1.CA - CB-8039 Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project.

briefs.title: Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved a contract for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project. This project involves replacing a drainage structure (culvert) and repaving the area around it.
briefs.source_body:
7.11
CA - (CB-3264) CENTRAL VALLEY GREENWAY
CULVERT REPLACEMENT AND PAVING WORKS
[image]
1.CA - (CB-3264) Central Valley Greenway Culvert
Replacement 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and
Paving Project.

briefs.title: Cellular Service Contract Change Requires Council Approval
briefs.summary: A change to the city's cellular voice and data services contract needs approval from the City Council. This change affects the city's existing agreement for telephone and data services.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. This is for informational purposes.
briefs.source_body:
7.12
CC - TELEPHONY - CELLULAR VOICE AND DATA
SERVICES
[image]
1.CC - Telephony - Cellular Voice and Data Services
2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (Telephony) 2026-04-14
.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Council Report dated September 25, 2023
(Telephony) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change
(CC) for Telephony - Cellular Voice and Data Services.

briefs.title: HVAC Maintenance Contract Change Approval Sought
briefs.summary: The city council is being asked to approve a change to the contract for heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) system maintenance and repair services at city-owned buildings. This ensures these systems continue to function properly.
briefs.source_body:
7.13
CC - HVAC SYSTEM MAINTENANCE & REPAIR
SERVICES
[image]
1.CC - HVAC System Maint. and Repair Services
2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (HVAC System) 2026-04-14
.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Previous Council Report dated May 27, 2025
(HVAC System) 2026-04-14 .pdf

Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a contract change (CC)
for HVAC system maintenance & repair services at various City-
owned buildings.

briefs.title: Cisco WebEx Contact Centre License Renewal Approved
briefs.summary: The council approved renewing the contract for the Cisco WebEx Contact Centre License Subscription. This ensures continued access to the WebEx platform for contact centre operations.
briefs.source_body:
7.14
CR - CISCO WEBEX CONTACT CENTER LICENSE
SUBSCRIPTION
[image]
1.CR - Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscriptions
2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (Cisco) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal
(CR) for Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription.

briefs.title: Fire Protection Contract Renewal Approval Sought
briefs.summary: A contract renewal for fire protection systems and equipment installation, maintenance, and repair services is up for approval. This ensures these services continue without interruption.
briefs.source_body:
7.15
CR - FIRE PROTECTION SYSTEMS AND
EQUIPMENT INSTALLATION MAINTENANCE AND
REPAIR SERVICES
[image]
1.CR – Fire Protection Systems and Equipment Installation
Mtnce and Repair Services 2026-04-14 .pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (Fire Services) 2026-04-14
.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal
(CR) for Fire Protection Systems and Equipment Installation
Maintenance and Repair Services.

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Overpass Plaque Wording: Council Approval Needed
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council needs to approve the wording on a plaque for the Burnaby Lake Overpass opening. The plaque will be installed as part of the overpass opening.
briefs.source_body:
7.16
BURNABY LAKE OVERPASS OFFICIAL OPENING
PLAQUE
[image]
1.Burnaby Lake Overpass Official Opening Plaque
2026-04-14.pdf

2.Attachment 1 - Burnaby Lake Overpass Plaque
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for the wording and
content for the commemorative plaque to be installed as part of
the opening of Burnaby Lake Overpass.

briefs.title: City of Burnaby to Review Financial Practices
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby will hire an external reviewer. This reviewer will check the city's financial practices. The goal is to protect the city's money and prevent large tax increases or service cost increases for businesses and residents.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL
PRACTICES
[image]
1.FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to engage an external
reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial
standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases
and rising service costs for businesses and residents.

briefs.title: Burnaby Seeks Approval for Beresford Street 20-Storey Apartment Development
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby is seeking Council approval to move forward with a proposal to build a 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street. This proposal, REZ #25-11, is part of the Royal Oak Community Plan. The City Council will hold First and Second Readings at a future meeting.
briefs.action_required: Monitor City Council meetings for updates on REZ #25-11.
briefs.source_body:
8.2
PDC - REZ #25-11 - 5035 BERESFORD STREET -
PROPOSED 20-STOREY APARTMENT
DEVELOPMENT (ROYAL OAK COMMUNITY PLAN)
[image]
1.PDC- REZ 25-11 5053 Beresford Street 2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - REZ 25- 11 - Part 1 of 3 - Sketch 1 and 2
2026-04-14.pdf

3.Attachment 1 - REZ 25-11 - Part 2 of 3 - Shadow Study, Site
Plan and Subdivision Plan 2026-04-14.pdf
4.Attachment 1 - REZ 25-11 - Part 3 of 3 - Front Elevation,
West and East Elevations 2026-04-14.pdf
5.Attachment 2 - REZ 25-11 - Development Statistics
2026-04-14.pdf
6.Attachment 3 - REZ 25-11 - Rezoning Prerequisites
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-11
to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

briefs.title: Riverbend Business Park Developer Forfeits Amenity Pier Security
briefs.summary: The developer of 8351 Fraser Reach Court in the Riverbend Business Park will not build the planned public amenity pier. Instead, they are giving up the security deposit they had provided to the City of Burnaby. The City will hold this money and decide later how it will be used.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
AMENITY PIER COVENANT - DEVELOPER
ELECTION TO FORFEIT SECURITY
[image]
1.Amenity Pier Covenant Developer Election to Forfeit Security
2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant)
2026-04-14.pdf

Purpose: To provide Council with information regarding the
owner’s election under a Section 219 Covenant registered on
title at 8351 Fraser Reach Court to forfeit the posted security
and not proceed with construction of the public amenity pier
contemplated under the Riverbend Business Park rezoning
approvals, and the City’s holding of the forfeited security
pending future Council direction.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) Bylaw Rejected
briefs.summary: A proposed bylaw to designate the Burnaby North Road area as a Business Improvement Area (BIA) will not proceed. Sufficient petitions against the BIA were received, halting its creation.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14797 - Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby
North Road) Bylaw 2026
[image]
1.14797 Burnaby BIA (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026
(FA).pdf
Purpose: to designate the area of the Burnaby North Road
Business Improvement Association

(Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council February 24, 2026)
see Item 7.2.
NOTE: sufficient petitions against the proposed BIA have been
received

briefs.title: Burnaby Awards Bursaries to Local Secondary School Students
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby is giving bursary awards to secondary school students. The awards will be announced at the April 14, 2026, Open Council meeting.

key_dates.label: Bursary award recipients announced at Open Council meeting

briefs.source_body:
14.1
Resolution No. C2026 - 033

THAT a bursary award be granted to the following secondary
school students:
• Juliana Marie San Mateo - École Alpha
• Vienna Dias - Burnaby Central
• Ronia Rameshi - Burnaby Mountain
• Erin Curley - Burnaby North
• Yolanda Wu - Burnaby South
• Malak Albadry - Byrne Creek
• Hayden Young Crawford - École Cariboo Hill
• Yunshu (Isabella) Liao - Moscrop
THAT staff be authorized to contact recipients and secondary
school principals to advise them of the award; and
THAT the award recipients be announced at the April 14, 2026
Open Council meeting.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
